lines.english: Lesson 57
lines.chinese: 第57课
lines.raw: Lesson 57 | 第57课

lines.english: Back in the old country
lines.chinese: 回到故乡
lines.raw: Back in the old country | 回到故乡

lines.english: Did the narrater find his mother's grave?
lines.chinese: 叙述者找到他母亲的坟墓了吗？
lines.raw: Did the narrater find his mother's grave? | 叙述者找到他母亲的坟墓了吗？

lines.english: I stopped to let the car cool off and to study the map.
lines.chinese: 我停车让车冷却下来，并研究地图。
lines.raw: I stopped to let the car cool off and to study the map. | 我停车让车冷却下来，并研究地图。

lines.english: I had expected to be near my objective by now, but everything still seemed alien to me.
lines.chinese: 我本以为现在应该离目的地很近了，但周围的一切对我来说仍然很陌生。
lines.raw: I had expected to be near my objective by now, but everything still seemed alien to me. | 我本以为现在应该离目的地很近了，但周围的一切对我来说仍然很陌生。

lines.english: I was only five when my father had taken me abroad, and that was eighteen years ago.
lines.chinese: 我父亲带我出国时我才五岁，那已经是十八年前的事了。
lines.raw: I was only five when my father had taken me abroad, and that was eighteen years ago. | 我父亲带我出国时我才五岁，那已经是十八年前的事了。

lines.english: When my mother had died after a tragic accident, he did not quickly recover from the shock and loneliness.
lines.chinese: 母亲在一场悲惨的事故中去世后，他未能很快从震惊和孤独中恢复过来。
lines.raw: When my mother had died after a tragic accident, he did not quickly recover from the shock and loneliness. | 母亲在一场悲惨的事故中去世后，他未能很快从震惊和孤独中恢复过来。

lines.english: Everything around him was full of her presence, continually reopening the wound.
lines.chinese: 他周围的一切都充满了她的气息，不断地撕开他的伤口。
lines.raw: Everything around him was full of her presence, continually reopening the wound. | 他周围的一切都充满了她的气息，不断地撕开他的伤口。

lines.english: So he decided to emigrate.
lines.chinese: 所以他决定移民。
lines.raw: So he decided to emigrate. | 所以他决定移民。

lines.english: In the new country he became absorbed in making a new life for the two of us, so that he gradually ceased to grieve.
lines.chinese: 在新的国家，他全心投入为我们俩创造新生活，于是他渐渐停止了悲伤。
lines.raw: In the new country he became absorbed in making a new life for the two of us, so that he gradually ceased to grieve. | 在新的国家，他全心投入为我们俩创造新生活，于是他渐渐停止了悲伤。

lines.english: He did not marry again and I was brought up without a woman's care; but I lacked for nothing, for he was both father and mother to me.
lines.chinese: 他没有再婚，我是在没有女性关爱的情况下长大的；但我什么都不缺，因为他既是我的父亲也是我的母亲。
lines.raw: He did not marry again and I was brought up without a woman's care; but I lacked for nothing, for he was both father and mother to me. | 他没有再婚，我是在没有女性关爱的情况下长大的；但我什么都不缺，因为他既是我的父亲也是我的母亲。

lines.english: He always meant to go back one day, but not to stay.
lines.chinese: 他一直打算有一天回去，但不是为了留下。
lines.raw: He always meant to go back one day, but not to stay. | 他一直打算有一天回去，但不是为了留下。

lines.english: His roots and mine had become too firmly embedded in the new land.
lines.chinese: 他和我的根基都已在新土地上扎得太深了。
lines.raw: His roots and mine had become too firmly embedded in the new land. | 他和我的根基都已在新土地上扎得太深了。

lines.english: But he wanted to see the old folk again and to visit my mother's grave.
lines.chinese: 但他想再见见老乡们，并去我母亲的坟墓看看。
lines.raw: But he wanted to see the old folk again and to visit my mother's grave. | 但他想再见见老乡们，并去我母亲的坟墓看看。

lines.english: He became mortally ill a few months before we had planned to go and, when he knew that he was dying, he made me promise to go on my own.
lines.chinese: 在我们计划动身前的几个月，他得了绝症，当他知道自己快要去世时，他让我答应独自前往。
lines.raw: He became mortally ill a few months before we had planned to go and, when he knew that he was dying, he made me promise to go on my own. | 在我们计划动身前的几个月，他得了绝症，当他知道自己快要去世时，他让我答应独自前往。

lines.english: I hired a car the day after landing and bought a comprehensive book of maps,
lines.chinese: 降落的第二天，我租了一辆车，买了一本详尽的地图册，
lines.raw: I hired a car the day after landing and bought a comprehensive book of maps, | 降落的第二天，我租了一辆车，买了一本详尽的地图册，

lines.english: which I found most helpful on the cross-country journey, but which I did not think I should need on the last stage.
lines.chinese: 我发现这本地图册在跨国旅途中非常有用，但我不认为在最后一段路程中我还需要它。
lines.raw: which I found most helpful on the cross-country journey, but which I did not think I should need on the last stage. | 我发现这本地图册在跨国旅途中非常有用，但我不认为在最后一段路程中我还需要它。

lines.english: It was not that I actually remembered anything at all.
lines.chinese: 并不是我真的记得任何东西。
lines.raw: It was not that I actually remembered anything at all. | 并不是我真的记得任何东西。

lines.english: But my father had described over and over again what we should see at every milestone after leaving the nearest town,
lines.chinese: 但父亲曾一遍又一遍地描述，离开最近的城镇后，每过一个里程碑我们会看到什么，
lines.raw: But my father had described over and over again what we should see at every milestone after leaving the nearest town, | 但父亲曾一遍又一遍地描述，离开最近的城镇后，每过一个里程碑我们会看到什么，

lines.english: so that I was positive I should recognize it as familiar territory.
lines.chinese: 所以我确信我会认出那是熟悉的土地。
lines.raw: so that I was positive I should recognize it as familiar territory. | 所以我确信我会认出那是熟悉的土地。

lines.english: Well, I had been wrong, for I was now lost.
lines.chinese: 嗯，我错了，因为我现在迷路了。
lines.raw: Well, I had been wrong, for I was now lost. | 嗯，我错了，因为我现在迷路了。

lines.english: I looked at the map and then at the milometer.
lines.chinese: 我看了看地图，然后看了看里程表。
lines.raw: I looked at the map and then at the milometer. | 我看了看地图，然后看了看里程表。

lines.english: I had come ten miles since leaving the town and at this point, according to my father,
lines.chinese: 我离开城镇已经走了十英里，按照我父亲的说法，在这个地方，
lines.raw: I had come ten miles since leaving the town and at this point, according to my father, | 我离开城镇已经走了十英里，按照我父亲的说法，在这个地方，

lines.english: I should be looking at farms and cottages in a valley,
lines.chinese: 我应该能看到山谷里的农场和小屋，
lines.raw: I should be looking at farms and cottages in a valley, | 我应该能看到山谷里的农场和小屋，

lines.english: with the spire of the church of our village showing in the far distance.
lines.chinese: 远处还能看到我们村教堂的尖顶。
lines.raw: with the spire of the church of our village showing in the far distance. | 远处还能看到我们村教堂的尖顶。

lines.english: I could see no valley, no farms, no cottages and no church spire-only a lake.
lines.chinese: 我看不到山谷，看不到农场，看不到小屋，也看不到教堂尖顶——只有一片湖。
lines.raw: I could see no valley, no farms, no cottages and no church spire-only a lake. | 我看不到山谷，看不到农场，看不到小屋，也看不到教堂尖顶——只有一片湖。

lines.english: I decided that I must have taken a wrong turning somewhere.
lines.chinese: 我断定我一定是在某个地方拐错了弯。
lines.raw: I decided that I must have taken a wrong turning somewhere. | 我断定我一定是在某个地方拐错了弯。

lines.english: So I drove back to the town and began to retrace the route, taking frequent glances at the map.
lines.chinese: 于是我开车回到城镇，开始按原路返回，并不时地看一眼地图。
lines.raw: So I drove back to the town and began to retrace the route, taking frequent glances at the map. | 于是我开车回到城镇，开始按原路返回，并不时地看一眼地图。

lines.english: I landed up at the same corner.
lines.chinese: 我最终还是回到了同一个路口。
lines.raw: I landed up at the same corner. | 我最终还是回到了同一个路口。

lines.english: The curious thing was that the lake was not marked on the map.
lines.chinese: 奇怪的是，地图上并没有标示这片湖。
lines.raw: The curious thing was that the lake was not marked on the map. | 奇怪的是，地图上并没有标示这片湖。

lines.english: I felt as if I had stumbled into a nightmare country, as you sometimes do in dreams.
lines.chinese: 我感觉自己好像偶然闯入了一个噩梦般的国度，就像你在梦中有时会遇到的那样。
lines.raw: I felt as if I had stumbled into a nightmare country, as you sometimes do in dreams. | 我感觉自己好像偶然闯入了一个噩梦般的国度，就像你在梦中有时会遇到的那样。

lines.english: And, as in a nightmare, there was nobody in sight to help me.
lines.chinese: 就像在噩梦中一样，四周没有人能帮助我。
lines.raw: And, as in a nightmare, there was nobody in sight to help me. | 就像在噩梦中一样，四周没有人能帮助我。

lines.english: Fortunately for me, as I was wondering what to do next,
lines.chinese: 幸运的是，正当我不知下一步该怎么办的时候，
lines.raw: Fortunately for me, as I was wondering what to do next, | 幸运的是，正当我不知下一步该怎么办的时候，

lines.english: there appeared on the horizon a man on horseback, riding in my direction.
lines.chinese: 地平线上出现了一位骑马的人，正朝我这个方向骑来。
lines.raw: there appeared on the horizon a man on horseback, riding in my direction. | 地平线上出现了一位骑马的人，正朝我这个方向骑来。

lines.english: I waited till he came near, then I asked him the way to our old village.
lines.chinese: 我等到他走近，然后问他去我们那个老村子的路。
lines.raw: I waited till he came near, then I asked him the way to our old village. | 我等到他走近，然后问他去我们那个老村子的路。

lines.english: He said that there was now no village.
lines.chinese: 他说现在已经没有那个村子了。
lines.raw: He said that there was now no village. | 他说现在已经没有那个村子了。

lines.english: I thought he must have misunderstood me, so I repeated its name.
lines.chinese: 我以为他一定是误解了我的意思，所以我又重复了一遍村子的名字。
lines.raw: I thought he must have misunderstood me, so I repeated its name. | 我以为他一定是误解了我的意思，所以我又重复了一遍村子的名字。

lines.english: This time he pointed to the lake.
lines.chinese: 这次他指向了湖面。
lines.raw: This time he pointed to the lake. | 这次他指向了湖面。

lines.english: The village no longer existed because it had been submerged, and all the valley too.
lines.chinese: 那个村子已经不复存在了，因为它连同整个山谷都被淹没了。
lines.raw: The village no longer existed because it had been submerged, and all the valley too. | 那个村子已经不复存在了，因为它连同整个山谷都被淹没了。

lines.english: The lake was not a natural one, but a man-made reservoir.
lines.chinese: 那片湖不是天然形成的，而是一个人工水库。
lines.raw: The lake was not a natural one, but a man-made reservoir. | 那片湖不是天然形成的，而是一个人工水库。
